No crypto Package, No Node.js — The Web Crypto API Does It All in the Browser

Sooner or later you'll need to hash, encrypt, or decrypt data in the browser. In the past that meant Node.js's crypto module or a third-party package like crypto-js. But modern browsers already ship the Web Crypto API, so you can do all of it with zero dependencies. This post walks through hashing, AES encryption/decryption, HMAC signing, RSA, and a few handy helpers.
